english: 2. class or region of birth or re-birth, class of beings (v. gró-ba ('gro ba)); byol sóṅ-gi skye-ba (byol song gi skye ba) the being born as an animal 3. manner of birth उपपाद, skyé-ba bżi (skye ba bzhi), also चतुर्योनि the four kinds or ways of being born: mṅál-las (mngal las) (or nas (nas)) out of a womb (so, acc. to Stg., elephants and some men are born), sgo-ṅá-las (sgo nga las) out of an egg (birds, some klu (klu), some men), drod-gśér-las (drod gsher las) out of heat and humidity (insects, some men etc.), rdzús-te (rdzus te) in a supernatural way (so the lha (lha), the Buddhas, when they spring from lotus-flowers; also the inhabitants of infernal regions, souls in the bardo and some men). — skye-gzúgs (skye gzugs) prob. = byad-gzúgs (byad gzugs) stature, figure. — skye-rábs (skye rabs) series of the births of a man, history of them, and esp. so of the births of Buddha, — so in the title of a work. — skye-śiṅ (skye shing) = skyed-śiṅ (skyed shing) Wdn.

1. being, (animans) mi-la-sogs-pa skyé-bo (mi la sogs pa skye bo) man and the other living beings Dzl. — 2. human being, man, gen. as a collective noun: mankind, khrúl-bcas skyé-bo ('khrul bcas skye bo) infatuated men Pth.; skyé-bo mkás-pa gżan-rnams (skye bo mkhas pa gzhan rnams) other sensible people Tar.; skyé-bo maṅ-poi yid-du ḥóṅ-ba (skye bo mang po'i yid du 'ong ba) universally beloved Dom.; mi nag skyé-bo (mi nag skye bo) laymen (on account of the dimness of their religious knowledge); so-sói skyé-bo (so so'i skye bo) पृथग्जन (cf. Will.) the lower clergy, common monks Tar., but also simple laymen, if they are not quite without religious knowledge; skye-bo-cóg (skye bo cog), (skyeo-cóg (skye'o cog) Cs. is a less accurate pronunciation), skyé-dgú (skye dgu), or (less correctly) rgu (rgu), men, mankind; skye-dgui-bdág-mo (skye dgu'i bdag mo) प्रजापती fem. pr. n., the aunt and first governess of Buddha Glr., Gyatch., also a name of dpal-lhá-mo's (dpal lha mo's) q.v.
Cs.: = kég (keg), kag (kag) misfortune. But rtsis-kyi skyeg (rtsis kyi skyeg) Lex. w.e.?
1. n. of a bird: čú-sky. (chu sky.) Lex. w.e., Sch.: coot, water-hen; ri-skyegs (ri skyegs) Lex. w.e., Cs.: a large singing-bird, Sch.; grouse, heath-cock. — 2. rgya-skyégs (rgya skyegs) shell-lac.
and skyeṅs-pa (skyengs pa) to be ashamed, - also ṅo-skyeṅ-ba (ngo skyeng ba), B. and col. frq.
also skye- (skye-) or skya-ser-rluṅ (skya ser rlung) Mil., cold wind.

and skye (skye), 1. growth, increase, skyed če-bar gyur-ba (skyed che bar gyur ba) to grow much; gżan-gyi zla-skyed-pas deí żag-skyed če (gzhan gyi zla skyed pas de'i zhag skyed che) his daily growth was greater than the growth of others in a month etc. Pth. — 2. progress, the getting on, improvement skyed yoṅ (skyed yong) progress comes, I am making progress Mil.; profit, gain nad-la skyed med (nad la skyed med) (this) is of no use for that disease, of no benefit S.g. fol. 10. — 3. interest C., dṅul-skyed (dngul skyed) of money, ḥbru-skyed ('bru skyed) of corn C., skyed-du gtoṅ-ba (skyed du gtong ba) to give on interest Cs.; skyed póg-pa (skyed phog pa) Cs.: 'to be the full term of payment', more accurately: skyed póg (skyed phog) I (you, he etc.) am struck or hit by the term of payment; skyed-cȧn (skyed can) yielding interest, profit Cs.
Mil.nt. prob. = rgyal-sgo (rgyal sgo) principal door.

I. vb pf. bskyed (bskyed), act. to skye-ba (skye ba), in W. pronounced alike: *skye-ce* (*skye ce*) 1. to generate, procreate; seldom in a physical sense: bskyed-paí yab (bskyed pa'i yab) ὁ γεννήσας πατήρ Pth., (opp. to bltams-paí yum (bltams pa'i yum) Pth., for which however skyed-ma (skyed ma) Cs. does not seem to be an appropriate substitute). — 2. to produce, form, cause (opp. to med-par byed-pa (med par byed pa) to destroy, annihilate) e. g. diseases, fear, roots of virtue, merit, bsod-nams-kyi tsogs (bsod nams kyi tshogs), sa-bon (sa bon) (fig.) Dzl., ḥbras-bu ('bras bu) retribution; to reproduce, zad-pa (zad pa) what has been consumed Med.; to create certain thoughts or affections either in one's self or in others: spró-ba bskyed-pas deí pa-ma yaṅ spró-ba cúṅ-zad skyés-nas (spro ba bskyed pas de'i pha ma yang spro ba cung zad skyes nas) by his own rejoicing also to his parents a little joy arising Dzl. 22. 5; tams-cȧd-kyis brtson-ḥgrús bskyed-do (thams cad kyis brtson 'grus bskyed do) they all created zeal, took great pains Dzl.; ces bsȧm-pa bskyed-nas (ces bsam pa bskyed nas) thus they thought. — 3. to cause to germinate or grow, yúr-baí čú-yis żiṅ skyed (yur ba'i chu yis zhing skyed)
